vulnerability.title: Add nodev Option to /boot
vulnerability.description: The nodev mount option can be used to prevent device files from being created in /boot. Legitimate character and block devices should exist only in the /dev directory on the root partition or within chroot jails built for system services. Add the nodev option to the fourth column of /etc/fstab for the line which controls mounting of /boot.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if ( ! ( { rpm --quiet -q kernel-core ;} && { rpm --quiet -q rpm-ostree ;} && { rpm --quiet -q bootc ;} && { ! rpm --quiet -q openshift-kubelet ;} && ([ -f /run/ostree-booted ] || [ -L /ostree ]) ) && ! ( [ -f /.dockerenv ] || [ -f /run/.containerenv ] ) ); then function perform_remediation { # the mount point /boot has to be defined in /etc/fstab # before this remediation can be executed. In case it is not defined, the # remediation aborts and no changes regarding the mount point are done. mount_point_match_regexp="$(printf "^[[:space:]]*[^#].*[[:space:]]%s[[:space:]]" "/boot")" grep "$mount_point_match_regexp" -q /etc/fstab \ || { echo "The mount point '/boot' is not even in /etc/fstab, so we can't set up mount options" >&2; echo "Not

triage_decision.reason: Adding the nodev mount option to /boot is a simple configuration edit that prevents device files from being created in /boot. It does not modify the bootloader, partition tables, or critical services, and is unlikely to cause lockout or system breakage.

execution_details.stdout:
#
# /etc/fstab
# Created by anaconda on Mon Feb  9 07:40:38 2026
#
# Accessible filesystems, by reference, are maintained under '/dev/disk/'.
# See man pages fstab(5), findfs(8), mount(8) and/or blkid(8) for more info.
#
# After editing this file, run 'systemctl daemon-reload' to update systemd
# units generated from this file.
#
/dev/mapper/rlm-root    /                       xfs     defaults        0 0
UUID=8bbec118-7baf-40c6-910b-6a336e901242 /boot xfs     defaults        0 0
UUID=F56C-228D          /boot/efi               vfat    umask=0077,shortname=winnt 0 2
/dev/mapper/rlm-home    /home                   xfs     defaults        0 0
/dev/mapper/rlm-swap    none                    swap    defaults        0 0
tmpfs /dev/shm tmpfs defaults 0 0

#
# /etc/fstab
# Created by anaconda on Mon Feb  9 07:40:38 2026
#
# Accessible filesystems, by reference, are maintained under '/dev/disk/'.
# See man pages fstab(5), findfs(8), mount(8) and/or blkid(8) for more info.
#
# After editing this file, run 'systemctl daemon-reload' to update systemd
# units generated from this file.
#
/dev/mapper/rlm-root    /                       xfs     defaults        0 0
UUID=8bbec118-7baf-40c6-910b-6a336e901242 /boot xfs     defaults,nodev        0 0
UUID=F56C-228D          /boot/efi               vfat    umask=0077,shortname=winnt 0 2
/dev/mapper/rlm-home    /home                   xfs     defaults        0 0
/dev/mapper/rlm-swap    none                    swap    defaults        0 0
tmpfs /dev/shm tmpfs defaults 0 0

llm_verdict.message:
The **nodev** mount option has been added to the **/boot** entry in `/etc/fstab`.

**Updated line in `/etc/fstab`**

```text
UUID=8bbec118-7baf-40c6-910b-6a336e901242 /boot xfs     defaults,nodev        0 0
```

All other lines remain unchanged and no duplicate entries were introduced.

**Optional – apply immediately**

```bash
# Remount /boot to activate the new option
mount -o remount /boot

# Verify the mount options for /boot
findmnt -no OPTIONS /boot
```

The OpenSCAP finding **mount_option_boot_nodev** is now resolved.

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
Plan: read /etc/fstab, locate the line whose mount point is /boot. Extract the options column (fourth field). If it already contains options, append “nodev” unless it’s present; if it’s empty, set it to “nodev”. Use a single in‑place sed substitution to perform the change, ensuring no duplicate lines are created. Then run the plan after review.

Read `/etc/fstab` and locate the line that mounts `/boot`.  
Use `sed` to edit that line in place:  
- Match the pattern `(^[^#]*\s+/boot\s+[^ ]+\s+)([^ ]+)(.*)` which captures the mount point and its options field.  
- If the line does not already contain `nodev`, insert `nodev` into the options field: `\1\2,nodev\3`.  
- The command `sed -i -E '/\s\/boot\s/ { /nodev/! s|(^[^#]*\s+/boot\s+[^ ]+\s+)([^ ]+)(.*)|\1\2,nodev\3| }' /etc/fstab` ensures no duplication and only modifies the relevant line.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan correctly adds the nodev option to the /boot entry in /etc/fstab, which resolves the vulnerability. Minor improvements such as backing up the file and remounting /boot can be added.
concerns: Changes are not applied immediately; a remount or reboot is required to enforce the option
The sed command may add nodev even if the options field is empty or contains a placeholder
suggested_improvements: Create a backup of /etc/fstab (e.g., cp /etc/fstab /etc/fstab.bak) before editing
After editing, run 'mount -o remount /boot' or 'findmnt -no OPTIONS /boot' to verify the option is active
Consider using a more precise regex or awk to avoid false positives and ensure idempotency
